title: Provider Routing
summary: How to configure fine-grained control over which underlying providers handle OpenRouter requests in Hermes, including sorting by cost/speed/quality, provider whitelists/blacklists, explicit ordering, parameter requirements, and data collection policies.

config_keys.description: Sort providers by: price | throughput | latency
Explicit priority order; unlisted providers are fallbacks
Only route to providers that support all request parameters

code_examples.caption: Full provider routing config
code_examples.code:
provider_routing:
  sort: "price"
  only: []
  ignore: []
  order: []
  require_parameters: false
  data_collection: null

code_examples.caption: Single-provider auxiliary override (avoid OpenRouter fallback)
DeepSeek v4 Flash with required xhigh reasoning
code_examples.code:
model:
  name: deepseek-v4-flash
  reasoning: xhigh   # required for reliable tool calling

gotchas: Provider routing only applies when using OpenRouter — has no effect with direct provider connections
Append :nitro to any model name for throughput sorting (e.g., anthropic/claude-sonnet-4:nitro)
Append :floor to any model name for price sorting
Provider routing controls sub-providers within OpenRouter; for cross-provider failover use fallback-providers

Anthropic OAuth third-party harness usage routes to a separate `extra_usage` billing pool, NOT base subscription allowance (#15080); Claude Pro tier blocked entirely from this path — see community-anthropic-clawback-2026-06

Single-provider setups silently lose vision / web summarization / compression because auxiliary fallback chain defaults to OpenRouter first. Fix: add OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OR set `provider: "main"` in each auxiliary slot (vision, web_extract, compression)
DeepSeek v4 Flash is unreliable for tool calling unless `reasoning: xhigh` is set — undocumented requirement
DeepSeek v4 (any) has no vision capability (u/donnthebuilder: 'dead on arrival') — pair with a vision-capable aux model
GLM endpoint auto-probing: Hermes tries multiple GLM endpoints (global, China, coding variants) automatically — DO NOT pre-set GLM_BASE_URL, let it discover the working endpoint
full_text:
# Provider Routing

When using OpenRouter as your LLM provider, Hermes Agent supports provider routing — fine-grained control over which underlying AI providers handle your requests.
